title: R1 cmd_sha256 per-entry — silent-corruption gap closed
context: Meta-falsifier audit identified silent-corruption gap: anyone with write access to falsifiers.jsonl could replace `cmd` with `echo $PASS_TOKEN` (or sophisticated variants like `printf '%s\n' TOKEN`, `[ -f /etc/passwd ] && echo TOKEN`). R2 anti-spoof lint was applied (catches exact `^echo[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$` pattern only). R1 closes the gap completely via per-entry SHA256.

health_checker_patch.verification_logic: recompute sha256_16(cmd) per entry, compare to declared cmd_sha256; mismatch counted as ERROR with raw 66 trailer reason=cmd_sha256_mismatch fix=audit_registry_history
health_checker_patch.new_flag: --strict (R3 lite — registry-wide SHA256 vs pinned baseline; warn-only)

registry_baseline.purpose: optional pin for cron-CI; legitimate additive churn rotates the hash → warn-only by default

tier_1_artifacts.claim: 84 entries each carry cmd_sha256 (16-hex sha256 prefix of cmd)
verifies cmd_sha256 BEFORE executing cmd; mismatch → STATUS=TAMPERED with raw 66 trailer
registry-wide SHA256 baseline (R3 lite); --strict mode compares + warns

mutation_C_printf_spoof.cmd_before: grep -qE '^@P mu = mobius\(6\) = 1 :: foundation \[10\*\]' /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.n6 && echo MU_ANCHOR_INTACT
mutation_C_printf_spoof.verdict: R1 strictly stronger than R2 — catches printf-class spoofs that R2 cannot

mutation_D_overt_update.verdict: by-design — overt change passes health, but git log records both diff lines (cmd + cmd_sha256), making the corruption traceable. raw 71 report-only respected — health check does not auto-revert.

bypassable_by: any cmd whose stdout contains the pass token

R1_cmd_sha256.scope: byte-exact equality of cmd via sha256
bypassable_by: overtly updating cmd_sha256 alongside cmd (traceable in git log)
R1_cmd_sha256.verdict: R1 reduces silent-corruption attack surface to overt git-traceable mutation

fixpoint.evidence: 84 entries hashed + checker patched + baseline pinned + 7/7 verification tests pass + Mutation C caught + Mutation D documented as by-design

next: R3 full: pin registry sha256 in state/, refresh on every legitimate edit (cron CI)
consider widening cmd_sha256 to 32-hex if collision risk grows
R4 candidate: cryptographic signature on registry (gpg/cosign) — defends against attacker who edits BOTH cmd and cmd_sha256
